title: About
text:
I am a PhD student in physics at Virginia Tech focused on quantum foundations and quantum error correction.
My work explores how classical objectivity and spacetime can emerge from quantum information-theoretic structure.

A Quantum Error-Correcting Perspective on Objectivity

title: Marin Girard
text:
Quantum foundations · Quantum information · Emergence


I study how classical objectivity and spacetime emerge from quantum information.

I am a PhD student in physics at Virginia Tech working on quantum foundations and quantum error correction, with an emphasis on the conceptual structure behind classicality, subsystems, and spacetime.
This site brings together my current research directions, selected papers, and CV.

title: Research
text: These are the main threads of the project at the moment. Even with a small number of active topics, the goal is to make the structure of the work legible quickly.

title: Research
text:
Quantum Foundations

A quantum error-correcting perspective on objectivity
Quantum mereology

This part of the work focuses on how classical structure can arise from intrinsically quantum descriptions, and on what counts as a subsystem in the first place.

title: Research
text:
Quantum Gravity and Information
How ideas from quantum information constrain the emergence of spacetime and the organization of degrees of freedom.
